discovery.summary: ROS 2 robot skill that follows a visible user with YOLO person tracking and stops when the arm camera detects a nearby edge.
discovery.language_representation: Use this package when the user wants the robot to come to them, follow the closest visible person, align to the person in the camera frame, drive forward at a low speed, publish web-viewable debug camera streams, and stop if the arm-mounted camera sees a table edge or boundary in the forward corridor.
capabilities: Detect people in the main camera with an Ultralytics YOLO model
Choose the largest visible person as the target
Rotate to center the target and drive forward at a low configurable speed
Use the arm camera to latch a stop when an edge enters the forward corridor
Publish YOLO, arm-edge, and combined debug views through web_video_server

notes: Requires the Python packages cv2, numpy, cv_bridge, rclpy, and ultralytics in the runtime environment.
Requires the maurice_msgs/srv/GotoJS interface package to be available in the ROS environment for the arm start-pose service.
GPU acceleration is optional; the script can run on CPU if the selected YOLO model is small enough.
Designed for the robot topic layout using /mars/main_camera/remote/left/image_raw, /mars/arm/image_raw, and /mars/arm/goto_js by default.
Requires a mobile base listening on the configured cmd_vel topic.

safety_requirements.motion_risk: commands mobile-base motion toward a person and must be supervised

come_to_user.summary: Start the robot person-following behavior that comes toward a visible user and stops on arm-camera edge detection.
come_to_user.description: Launch the come_to_user controller as a supervised long-running process.
come_to_user.language_representation: Use this target when the user asks the robot to come to them, follow them, approach a visible person, or demonstrate a person-following behavior with edge-stop safety. It first moves the arm to the configured start pose, then starts the long-running controller that detects people, centers the largest person, drives toward them slowly, publishes debug video topics, and latches stop on arm-camera edge detection.
examples: Make the robot come to the user.
Have the robot follow me and stop before the table edge.
Run the YOLO follow-me skill with the arm camera safety stop.

action_servers.name: come_to_user_controller
side_effects: Publishes Twist messages on the configured cmd_vel topic.
Launches web_video_server for browser-based debug viewing.
Keeps moving behavior active until the process is stopped or the edge stop latches.
Calls the configured arm start-pose service before enabling person following.
action_servers.language_representation: This is a long-running ROS process rather than a ROS action server. It calls the arm start-pose service, subscribes to the main and arm camera image topics, publishes debug image topics, commands the mobile base through cmd_vel, and launches web_video_server.

notes: Ultralytics must be installed in the Python environment used by ros2 run.
maurice_msgs/srv/GotoJS must be available in the ROS environment used by ros2 run.
Requires live main-camera and arm-camera image topics matching the configured topic names.
Requires the configured arm start-pose service.
Requires a mobile base listening on the configured cmd_vel topic.

safety_requirements.motion_risk: commands mobile-base motion toward a person and must be supervised

input_requirements.name: main_image_topic
input_requirements.description: Preferred main raw image topic used for YOLO person tracking.

input_requirements.name: arm_image_topic
input_requirements.description: Arm camera raw image topic used for edge detection.

input_requirements.name: cmd_vel_topic
input_requirements.description: Twist command topic for the mobile base.

input_requirements.name: model
input_requirements.description: Ultralytics YOLO model path or model name.

input_requirements.name: arm_start_service
input_requirements.description: Service used to move the robot arm to the start pose before following.

input_requirements.name: arm_start_service_type
input_requirements.description: ROS service type used by the arm start-pose service.

input_requirements.name: arm_start_duration_ms
input_requirements.description: Duration value sent to the arm start-pose service request.

input_requirements.name: arm_start_timeout
input_requirements.description: Seconds to wait for the arm start-pose service call before failing.

command: print('come_to_user controller was started by skill_acq. Keep --leave-processes-running enabled for this long-running robot behavior.')
